name: Thai Ridgeback
description: The Thai Ridgeback is one of only three breeds in the world with a distinctive ridge of hair running along its back in the opposite direction to the rest of its coat, and is the rarest of the three ridgeback breeds. An ancient primitive breed from Eastern Thailand, the Thai Ridgeback was used to hunt vermin, cobras, and other small game, as well as to pull carts and guard the home. Its isolated development in Thailand for thousands of years means this is a breed with extraordinarily pure genetics and strong primitive instincts.

conditions_detail.name: Hip Dysplasia
conditions_detail.description: Hip dysplasia is a genetic condition where the hip joint doesn't develop properly, causing the ball and socket to not fit together correctly. This leads to joint deterioration, pain, and arthritis over time.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Treatment ranges from weight management and physical therapy for mild cases to total hip replacement for severe cases. NSAIDs and joint supplements help manage pain.
prevention_tips: Screen breeding stock with hip scoring
